summarization.a: Panel (a) shows the growth rate increases with precursor temperature and plateaus at ~0.045 Å/cycle above 60 °C.
summarization.b: Panel (b) demonstrates that increasing pulse time beyond 2 s does not further increase the growth rate, indicating saturation behavior.
summarization.c: Panel (c) shows a similar saturation with hydrogen pressure—growth rate reaches a maximum of ~0.045 Å/cycle at 2 atm.
summarization.d: Panel (d) highlights the effect of H₂ dose, where a minimum threshold is needed to initiate growth, followed by saturation. These trends collectively illustrate the self-limiting surface reactions characteristic of ALD processes.

b.question: List two parameters that, when increased beyond a threshold, do not further enhance the growth rate

a.question: Is there evidence of a self-limiting growth process in the graphs?
What is the approximate saturated growth rate for cobalt films in these experiments

c.question: Why does the growth rate of cobalt films plateau in each panel despite continued increases in precursor temperature, pulse time, H₂ pressure, or H₂ dose?
c.answer: The plateau observed in each panel reflects the self-limiting nature of atomic layer deposition (ALD). In ALD, once all available reactive sites on the substrate surface are saturated during a precursor pulse, further increases in precursor supply (by time, dose, or temperature) do not result in additional growth. This ensures precise thickness control and uniform film coverage. For example, once sufficient H₂ pressure or dose is supplied to reduce surface intermediates, additional hydrogen does not increase growth, maintaining a constant growth per cycle.
